name: omarchy_navigation
content:
Navigation 
Everything in Omarchy happens via the keyboard — EVERYTHING! When the system first
starts, you iteray can't do a thing with the mouse aone. But you can hit Super + Space
to revea the appication auncher and Super + Alt + Space to open the Omarchy Menu.
These two commands aow you to do just about everything.
But the appication auncher is not intended to be the main way to operate the system
most of the time. We can get faster than that! A the most important appications are
bound directy to individua hotkeys. You start the termina with Super + Return  and a
browser with Super + Shift + B. Try doing one after the other, and you' see the magic
of Hyprand's tiing in action:
You can then hit Super + J  to stack them horizontay instead of verticay:
Navigation · The Omarchy Manual · DHH https://learn.omacom.io/2/the-omarchy-manual/51/navigation
1 of 4 2/2/26, 1:23 AM

---

Hit Super + J  again to return them to horizonta positions. Then try Super + Shift +
Arrow Right whie on the browser to swap the windows.
Now try Super + Ctrl + T to start the Activity monitor. That' appears as a foating
window. You can tie it using Super + T  (and hit that again to make it foating again). Now
press Super + Shift + F to open the fies manager. You' have a neat four-way setup:
You navigate between the window you want to be active with Super + Arrow . This wi
switch focus and move the cursor to the center of the new appication.
Navigation · The Omarchy Manual · DHH https://learn.omacom.io/2/the-omarchy-manual/51/navigation
2 of 4 2/2/26, 1:23 AM

---

switch focus and move the cursor to the center of the new appication.
If you hit Super + Shift + 2, you' move the current focused appication onto the second
workspace. Super + Shift + 1 moves it back. And Super + Shift + Alt + 2 wi move
the current focused appication onto the second workspace without switching to it).
If you hod down Super  and use the mouse to cick on a window, you' be abe to
rearrange where it sits. If you hod Super  and use the right button on the mouse, you can
freey resize the window.
You cose a window on Super + W  (and cose a windows on Ctrl + Alt + Delete).
You can aso go fu screen with Super + F  or even just fu-width (keeping the top bar)
with Super + Alt + F.
Grouping windows 
Windows can be grouped using Super + G . Once you're in a group, every window you
start whie that's active wi beong to the group. You can move between these grouped
windows using Super + Ctrl + Arrows or Super + Alt + 1/2/3/4 to go directy to
grouped window in order.
You can move a window out of the grouping with Super + Alt + G or disassembe the
entire group by hitting Super + G  again. Finay, you can move windows outside the group
into it with Super + Alt + Arrows.
Scratchpad workspace 
Finay, there's a specia scratchpad workspace that overays on whatever workspace
you're currenty on. You access what's on that using Super + S  and you pace windows
there using Super + Alt + S.
It works we for contros or perhaps a termina that you quicky want to interact with in an
overay without eaving the current workspace. If you want to move a window off the
scratchpad, you just move it directy to another workspace with something ike Super +
Shift + 1 to move it to workspace 1.
It takes some getting used to! 
It takes a itte whie to get used to navigating your desktop ike this, but once you do, it'

---

It takes a itte whie to get used to navigating your desktop ike this, but once you do, it'
be hard to go back to a traditiona mouse-driven desktop experience!
